game_name: Jordan vs. Bird: One on One

danger_signals.visual_form: Player avatar distance and court position relative to ball
danger_signals.meaning: Proximity to opposing player indicates defensive pressure and shot-blocking threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Opposing player sprite animation and body orientation
danger_signals.meaning: Active defensive posture signals increased difficulty in scoring or passing

safety_signals.visual_form: Unoccupied areas of the basketball court with no opponent sprite present
safety_signals.meaning: Available space for movement, shooting opportunity, or driving to basket

safety_signals.visual_form: Opposing player sprite positioned far from player avatar
safety_signals.meaning: Low defensive pressure allows for easier shot or ball control

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Court-based gameplay space represents Bulls home/away context
Court-based gameplay space represents Celtics home/away context

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two player sprites (Jordan vs. Bird) positioned on basketball court with ball
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Simplified game state with only two active entities; offensive and defensive roles alternate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Court changes to showcase arena with basket positioned for dunking opportunities
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jordan performs dunking animations; game evaluates air time and style

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Court layout emphasizes three-point line positioning and shooting stations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bird executes shooting animations from distance; game tracks successful three-pointers

environmental_storytelling.example: Jordan given slam dunk contest (reflects his athletic signature move); Bird given three-point contest (reflects his shooting prowess)
Chicago Bulls red vs. Boston Celtics green creates instant visual faction recognition and historical rivalry context
Basketball court layout communicates game rules through visual zones (three-point line, paint, baseline) that determine scoring and gameplay mechanics

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Sprite demonstrates dribbling and movement animations; capable of driving to basket or taking jump shots
Sprite adopts defensive stance; moves to intercept opponent or block shots
Sprite demonstrates shooting mechanics; specializes in three-point range and mid-range shots
Sprite adopts defensive positioning; moves to contest shots and defend three-point line

notes: Jordan vs. Bird: One on One is a two-player sports game with minimal world-state complexity. Visual communication relies primarily on sprite positioning, player animation states, and court layout. The game lacks traditional danger/faction conflict mechanics; instead, it manifests game-state through player-specific minigames and historical sports rivalry context. The NES port by Rare maintained core gameplay mechanics from the original 1988 versions.
